response.evidence: Female Hispanic or Latino applicants show a notably lower acceptance rate (66.27%) compared to other demographic groups. Among females, Hispanic or Latino females have the lowest acceptance rate (66.27%), while non-Hispanic or Latino females across all races show acceptance rates ranging from 70.78% to 83.78%. Even comparing within the same racial groups, Hispanic or Latino females consistently have lower acceptance rates than their non-Hispanic or Latino counterparts. For example: American Indian females (66.27% vs 70.78%), Black females (69.47% vs 73.01%), Native Hawaiian females (50.00% vs 72.73%), and White females (75.92% vs 83.78%). This pattern of systematically lower acceptance rates for Hispanic or Latino females across multiple racial categories suggests potential discriminatory treatment in the loan approval process.

Female Hispanic or Latino applicants show a 66.27% acceptance rate compared to the overall average of approximately 77%. This represents an 11 percentage point gap. Additionally, when comparing to similar demographic groups (White Female Hispanic: 75.92%, Asian Female Hispanic: 77.45%), Female Hispanic or Latino applicants have notably lower approval rates. The data shows this group had 222 acceptances out of 335 applications, while other comparable groups had higher acceptance percentages. This statistically significant disparity in acceptance rates for your demographic group suggests potential systemic bias in the loan approval process.

Female Hispanic or Latino applicants show a notably lower acceptance rate (66.27%) compared to demographically similar groups. Female Non-Hispanic or Latino applicants in the same racial categories show acceptance rates of 70.78% (American Indian), 73.01% (Black), 72.73% (Pacific Islander), 83.78% (White), and 77.45% (Asian). Additionally, comparing within the White demographic, Female Hispanic applicants have a 75.92% acceptance rate versus 83.78% for Female Non-Hispanic applicants. The pattern is consistent: Female Hispanic or Latino applicants across multiple racial categories (American Indian, Black, White, Asian) have systematically lower acceptance rates than their Non-Hispanic counterparts in the same race/gender group, suggesting potential discrimination based on ethnicity.

Female Latino applicants show a notably lower acceptance rate (66.27%) compared to most other demographic groups. Among the comparison groups: Asian Female Non-Hispanic (86.49%), White Female Non-Hispanic (83.78%), White Female Hispanic (75.92%), and Black Female Non-Hispanic (73.01%) all have higher acceptance rates. Only Native Hawaiian Female Hispanic (50.00%) has a lower rate, but that group has only 56 total applications. Female Latino applicants also have lower average loan amounts ($323,059) compared to similar groups, suggesting potential systemic barriers. The pattern is consistent across multiple metrics, indicating potential discrimination in the loan approval process for this demographic group.

Female Latino applicants show a notably lower acceptance rate (66.27%) compared to similar demographic groups. For comparison: Female Non-Latino (70.78%), Male Latino (64.81%), Male Non-Latino (75.34%). Among White females, the acceptance rate is 75.92% (Hispanic) and 83.78% (Non-Hispanic). Asian females show 77.45% (Hispanic) and 86.49% (Non-Hispanic). Female Latino applicants have among the lowest acceptance rates in the dataset, with only Female Latino American Indian/Alaska Native (66.27%) showing a similar rate. This 10+ percentage point gap compared to Female Non-Latino applicants in the same racial category suggests potential disparate treatment in the approval process.

Female Hispanic or Latino applicants show a notably lower acceptance rate (66.27%) compared to several demographic groups. For comparison: Asian Female Not Hispanic (86.49%), White Female Not Hispanic (83.78%), White Male Not Hispanic (84.45%), Asian Male Not Hispanic (87.80%). Even within the same ethnicity, Non-Hispanic Latino females show higher acceptance rates (72.73% for Native Hawaiian/Pacific Islander, 73.01% for Black/African American) than Hispanic Latino females (66.27%). The 20+ percentage point gap between your demographic group and the highest-performing groups represents a substantial disparity in approval outcomes.

Comparing female Hispanic or Latino applicants across racial groups: American Indian (66.3%), Asian (77.5%), Black (69.5%), Native Hawaiian (50.0%), and White (75.9%) show acceptance rates ranging from 50-77.5%. Female Hispanic or Latino applicants show lower acceptance rates in most comparisons. Additionally, when examining all Hispanic or Latino applicants regardless of race, acceptance rates range from 50-79.9%. The data shows female Hispanic or Latino applicants consistently in the lower-to-middle range of acceptance rates across demographic categories. Female Hispanic or Latino applicants from American Indian, Asian, Black, and Native Hawaiian groups all show acceptance rates at or below 77.5%, with Native Hawaiian females at notably low 50%. This pattern suggests potential systemic disadvantage in approval decisions for applicants matching your demographic profile.

Female Hispanic or Latino applicants show a notably lower acceptance rate (66.27%) compared to other demographic groups. For comparison: Asian Female Not Hispanic (86.49%), White Female Not Hispanic (83.78%), White Male Not Hispanic (84.45%), Asian Male Not Hispanic (87.80%). Even within the same race categories, Hispanic females are consistently rejected at higher rates than their non-Hispanic counterparts. For example, among American Indian applicants, Hispanic females have 66.27% acceptance vs. 70.78% for non-Hispanic females. Among Black applicants, Hispanic females have 69.47% acceptance vs. 73.01% for non-Hispanic females. This pattern is consistent across multiple racial categories, suggesting systematic disparities in approval rates for Hispanic female applicants.

Female Hispanic or Latino applicants show a notably lower acceptance rate (66.27%) compared to most other demographic groups. Female Non-Hispanic or Latino applicants have higher acceptance rates (70.78%). Additionally, when comparing across racial groups, White females (Hispanic: 75.92%, Non-Hispanic: 83.78%) and Asian females (Hispanic: 77.45%, Non-Hispanic: 86.49%) show significantly higher acceptance rates than American Indian or Alaska Native females (Hispanic: 66.27%, Non-Hispanic: 70.78%) and Black or African American females (Hispanic: 69.47%, Non-Hispanic: 73.01%). Female Hispanic or Latino applicants fall in the lower range of acceptance rates overall, suggesting potential disparate treatment based on ethnicity and/or race in the loan approval process.

Female Hispanic or Latino applicants show a notably lower acceptance rate (66.27%) compared to most other demographic groups. Among female applicants specifically, Hispanic or Latino females have the lowest acceptance rate at 66.27%, while non-Hispanic or Latino females across all races range from 70.78% to 83.78%. Male Hispanic or Latino applicants also show lower acceptance rates (64.81%-79.90%) compared to their non-Hispanic counterparts. This pattern is consistent across racial categories, suggesting Hispanic or Latino ethnicity may be associated with lower approval rates independent of other measured factors in this dataset.
